candidates.title: Negotiated Authority as Real-Estate Governance Model
candidates.shift: 18th-century Caribbean imperial governance shows authority as continuous negotiation between metropole, local elites, and institutions rather than top-down imposition.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: suggests future store/zoning governance in fragmented regulatory geographies (Gulf, Africa, Latin America) may function as ongoing negotiation, not fixed code - unsettling for standardized global retail rollout models.
candidates.evidence: Comparative thesis on Jamaica/Saint Domingue argues imperial authority was produced through ongoing negotiation, not simple imposition.

candidates.title: Disaster as Administrative Construct
candidates.shift: Historical flood analysis reframes disaster not as a natural event but as a sociopolitical failure of authority and administration.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: retail climate-resilience planning may need to model governance/accountability capacity, not just physical hazard exposure - disasters as absence-of-authority events.
candidates.evidence: 1934 Tiberias flood reframed as an administrative/authority failure that reshaped urban life in Mandate Palestine.

candidates.title: Autonomous Ground Robots Inherit Dangerous Labor
candidates.shift: Ukrainian frontline operations show ground robots and AI sensor networks absorbing the most hazardous physical tasks once done by humans.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: if ground robots absorb frontline risk work, similar logic could push physical AI into hazardous retail/warehouse tasks - disaster logistics, last-mile in unstable regions - a civilizational labor reallocation.
candidates.evidence: Ukrainian roboticists deploying AI-controlled ground robots and drones to remove humans from frontline zones.

candidates.title: The Absence of AI for Individualized Judgment
candidates.shift: Scholars argue AI is structurally unable to handle 'actor-specific' decisions - forward-looking, individual, non-generalizable choices.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: names a doctrine that doesn't yet exist - a formal limit on AI decisioning in retail contexts (hiring, personalized pricing, site selection) requiring permanent human judgment reserve.
candidates.evidence: Paper argues AI cannot handle actor-specific decision problems, defined as forward-looking and individual.

candidates.title: Monolingual Blind Spot in Autonomous AI Agents
candidates.shift: Long-horizon LLM agent benchmarks have implicitly assumed monolingual execution, ignoring multilingual real-world environments.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: global store operations spanning languages/markets could be undermined by AI agents silently failing across language boundaries - an absence in current AI infrastructure design.
candidates.evidence: New benchmark exposes that most agent benchmarks assume monolingual reasoning and tool-use pipelines.

candidates.title: Granularity Gap in Coastal Risk Models
candidates.shift: Coastal flood-risk assessment methods still lack reliable microscale/localized accuracy despite modeling advances.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: store and warehouse siting near coasts globally may rely on risk models systemically blind at the parcel level - a hidden infrastructure vulnerability for real estate planning.
candidates.evidence: Review finds persistent uncertainty in localized-impact accuracy across multihazard coastal risk assessment approaches.

candidates.title: Himalayan Micro-Adaptation as Retail Site Precedent
candidates.shift: A small Himalayan town develops localized climate adaptation strategies amid glacial and flood hazard exposure.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: peripheral-geography adaptation playbooks (terracing, water management) could inform future store/warehouse siting doctrine in climate-exposed non-Western mountain and valley regions.
candidates.evidence: Research proposes climate adaptation strategies specific to Kullu's hazard profile.

candidates.title: Biomass-Derived Nanomaterials Enter Structural Pipeline
candidates.shift: Bamboo's hierarchical architecture is being engineered into nanocellulose and lignin nanomaterials beyond its traditional structural use.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: could seed a new class of ultra-lightweight, bio-based structural or interior materials for stores in Asia-Pacific, displacing steel/plastic composites within a generation.
candidates.evidence: Review documents bamboo-derived nanocellulose and lignin nanomaterial fabrication advances beyond structural applications.

candidates.title: Humanoid Robots Cross Into High-Precision Physical Work
candidates.shift: US scientists used a Chinese-made humanoid robot to perform keyhole surgery, extending humanoid dexterity beyond controlled lab settings.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: cross-border humanoid deployment for precision tasks hints at future in-store humanoid labor (stocking, repair, fitting) sourced from non-Western robotics supply chains.
candidates.evidence: Chinese-made humanoid robot used by US scientists to perform organ-removal surgery via keyhole technique.

candidates.title: Industry 5.0's Re-Inclusion of Humans in Automation
candidates.shift: Industry 4.0's full-automation model is criticized for excluding humans from production; Industry 5.0 reframes robotics toward mandated human-robot collaboration.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: retail automation strategy may swing from a labor-replacement doctrine toward human-in-the-loop robotics requirements, reshaping store staffing models over the next decade.
candidates.evidence: Paper argues Industry 4.0 failed by excluding humans from production; Industry 5.0 reverses this via telecom-enabled collaborative robotics.

candidates.title: Obsolete Care Infrastructure Becomes Housing Stock
candidates.shift: A derelict care home in London is adaptively reused into a family dwelling, signaling re-purposing logic for aging institutional real estate.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: as demographic shifts hollow out care homes, schools, and other institutional buildings, similar adaptive-reuse logic could apply to obsolete big-box stores globally.
candidates.evidence: Formwork Architects converted a derelict Crouch End care home into a family home with a two-storey brick extension.

candidates.title: Childcare Embedded Into Work Campuses
candidates.shift: A university campus in Munich embeds a full childcare facility to support working parents on-site.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: suggests future retail/commercial real estate may need to co-locate care infrastructure to attract labor and shoppers, blurring store, community, and care building types.
candidates.evidence: Kéré Architecture's Kinderoase childcare facility built on the TUM campus in Munich for young professionals balancing work and family.

candidates.title: AI Interpretability Meets Super-App Consolidation
candidates.shift: Anthropic maps Claude's internal 'hidden space' of concepts while OpenAI moves toward a consolidated super-app interface.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: as AI assistants become interpretable and centralized, physical discovery and commerce could route entirely through one super-app, hollowing out the store's role as a discovery venue.
candidates.evidence: MIT Technology Review reports Anthropic's interpretability findings on Claude alongside OpenAI's super-app ambitions.

candidates.title: Zero-Staff Fulfillment Pods
candidates.shift: A fully robotic pharmacy pilot fills prescriptions with no on-site staff, claiming up to 96% lower fulfillment costs.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: if generalized beyond pharmacy, this unstaffed fulfillment-pod model could replace store-format retail entirely in other categories, not just drugs.
candidates.evidence: Palo Alto pilot: robotic system fills sealed wholesale bottles into verified prescriptions in 60 seconds with no staff on site.

candidates.title: Organics Diversion Mandate Expands Store Back-of-House
candidates.shift: NYC may broaden mandatory commercial composting requirements pending processing capacity and cost competitiveness.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: if this pattern spreads globally, store back-of-house design and operations may need dedicated organics infrastructure as baseline code, not an amenity.
candidates.evidence: NYC waste officials considering expanded organics diversion requirements based on processing capacity and cost versus landfill/incineration.
